_comment: GYF model registry — the machine-enforced license/lane gate (engineering-doctrine D2). Every model GYF can load is tagged here. CI (scripts/check_model_licenses.py) fails if any 'production' model is not commercial-clean + evaluated. RESEARCH-lane models are OFFLINE-only north-stars and never served. Keep in lockstep with tech-stack.md.

models.name: marqo-fashionSigLIP
models.notes: Shipped P1-A perception; superseded by google-siglip2-base at M2 (lost the bake-off on mrr/recall@5/recall@10). Kept as the commercial-clean fallback baseline behind the encoder port (invariant #5).

models.name: google-siglip2-base
models.notes: Promoted at M2. SigLIP 2 recipe, B/16, 768-dim (same width as the old incumbent => no vector migration). Won the M2 bake-off against marqo-fashionSigLIP on mrr/recall@5/recall@10; despite not being fashion-tuned, the SigLIP 2 training recipe wins. Served via local SiglipEncoder baseline or the ZeroGPU remote lane.

models.name: google-siglip2-so400m
models.notes: M2 bake-off candidate. Larger backbone (1152-dim → needs a vector-dim migration + re-embed if promoted). Only escalate to this if the base candidate is competitive. Research-lane until proven.

models.name: sam-3d-body
models.notes: Body-type estimator (M3, plan p1b-cycle2). Promote to production once M3 ships + eval attached + SAM License accepted.

models.name: mhr
models.notes: Parametric body model SAM 3D Body decodes into; SMPL-free. Promote with M3.

models.name: anny
models.train_data_license: Apache-2.0 / CC0 (MakeHuman); avoid the SMPL-X topology variant
models.notes: WHO-calibrated body model for measurement-threshold calibration + fairness (M3).

models.name: qwen3
models.notes: LLM upgrade for the NL goal box (currently rule-based parse_goal). Promote when wired behind IntentParser port.

models.name: fitdit
models.notes: North-star try-on quality, OFFLINE benchmark only. MUST NOT be served — see engineering-doctrine try-on path (use a licensed model at inference).

models.name: idm-vton
models.notes: Non-commercial try-on baseline; research-lane reference only.

models.name: retinaface-farl-celebm
models.license: MIT (facer package + FaRL code, both confirmed on FacePerceiver/facer and FacePerceiver/FaRL GitHub repos)
models.train_data_license: RetinaFace detector weights are trained on WIDER FACE, whose license restricts use to non-commercial research purposes — the exact 'MIT code, non-commercial weights' trap D2 exists to catch. FaRL/CelebM parser training-data terms not yet independently re-verified.
models.notes: CORRECTED (2026-07-07): was mistagged 'production' while failing its own gate (train_data_commercial_ok=false, eval_report=null) — that mistagging is what was turning CI red, not a code bug. This is the model actually running in FaceParsingSkinToneEstimator (ml/usermodel/skintone/estimator.py, model_version 'retinaface-farl-celebm-cielab-mst-v1'), but it is shadow-gated behind GYF_SKIN_TONE_ENABLED and never reaches the served ranking path today — 'research' lane accurately reflects that it is not commercial-clean-cleared for serving. Promote to production only after: (1) swap the WIDER-FACE-trained RetinaFace detector for a commercial-clean one (e.g. MediaPipe BlazeFace, verified viable in the 2026-07-06 research thread) or get a legal opinion on WIDER FACE terms, AND (2) it clears the skin-tone fairness eval (DoD gap 3.2 vs ≤1.0, see ml/usermodel/skintone/fairness_eval.py) with an attached eval_report.

models.name: birefnet-rtmw-bodyshape
models.license: MIT (BiRefNet, confirmed on ZhengPeng7/BiRefNet HF/GitHub) + Apache-2.0 (rtmlib, confirmed on Tau-J/rtmlib GitHub)
models.train_data_license: RTMW keypoints are trained on COCO-WholeBody (CC-BY-4.0 — commercial-OK). BiRefNet's matting head is trained on DIS5K, whose provenance/commercial terms have not yet been independently re-verified for this pairing — flagged conservatively rather than assumed clean.
models.notes: CORRECTED (2026-07-07): was mistagged 'production' while failing its own gate (train_data_commercial_ok=false, eval_report=null) — that mistagging is what was turning CI red, not a code bug. This is the model in SilhouetteBodyAdapter (ml/usermodel/body/estimator.py, DEFAULT_MODEL_VERSION 'rtmw-birefnet-v1'), but per CLAUDE.md §0.5 the remote GPU lane it needs is not wired on Render, so it abstains in prod rather than serving live. 'research' lane accurately reflects that it is not commercial-clean-cleared for serving. Promote to production only after DIS5K provenance is independently re-verified (or the matting head is swapped) AND it clears an attached eval_report (D5). sam-3d-body/mhr/anny above remain the research-lane north-star candidates for a future M3 replacement.
